title: Karnovsky’s Modified Fixative
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"64n63","text":"Fixative for mice and rat tissues to be used during perfusion and immersion fixation. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"74cf8","text":"Preparing Fix solution (2% paraformaldehyde and 2.5% glutaraldehyde)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"2sbn9","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"dji34","text":"For 100 mL of 2% paraformaldehyde and 2.5% glutaraldehyde in 0.15M cacodylate buffer solution at 7.4 pH.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"bq46u","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
before_start: {"blocks":[{"key":"nnjn","text":"Make sure heating plate for dissolving paraformaldehyde is between  60-70° C. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
materials_text: {"blocks":[{"key":"8mstn","text":"- 100mL Erlenmeyer flask","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"8040i","text":"- 100mL graduated cylinder","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"cnm2q","text":"- #1 Whatman filter ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"b5n0a","text":"- Paraformaldehyde PRILLS (Catalog no. 19202)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"engau","text":"- Glutaraldehyde EMS (Catalog no. 16220)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"bdlq6","text":"- Sodium Cacodylate buffer TED PELLA, INC. (Catalog no. 18851)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"2p92g","text":"- Stir bar","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Add 2.0 grams paraformaldehyde to a 100mL Erlenmeyer flask.
Add 35mL of DDH 2 O to 100mL to a Erlenmeyer flask.
Heat paraformaldehyde in DDH 2 O to 60-70ºC. Use stir bar to mix.
Filter solution with #1 Whatman filter into a 100mL graduated cylinder.
Dilute with DDH2O to a total volume 49.0 ml.
Add 1.0 ml of 0.204 M CaCl2-2H 2 O.
Add 50 ml 0.3M cacodylate buffer stock solution at pH 7.4.
Volume should be 100 ml. Transfer fixative into a 100mL Erlenmeyer flask.
creator: National Center for Microscopy and Imaging Research
